feed.title: Web Agent Bridge — Changelog
feed.subtitle: Open protocol for AI agent ↔ website interaction. Release notes and updates.

entry.title: v1.2.0 — WAB Protocol Ecosystem Release
entry.summary: Major release: WAB Protocol Specification v1.0, MCP Adapter for Claude/GPT integration, Fairness Engine for equitable AI discovery, Discovery Protocol with agent-bridge.json, WordPress plugin with full protocol support, dynamic subscription plan management, NoScript HTTP fallback for JS-disabled environments, Agent SDK with discover/ping/execute methods, and comprehensive OpenAPI documentation.
entry.content:
WAB Protocol v1.2.0 — Ecosystem Release

Protocol & Specification

  • WAB Protocol Specification v1.0 — Formal spec with Discovery, Command, Lifecycle, Security, and Fairness protocols
  • Discovery Protocol — Sites publish agent-bridge.json or /.well-known/wab.json
  • Command Protocol — Standardized JSON format for agent actions
  • Lifecycle Protocol — Discover → Authenticate → Plan → Execute → Confirm

MCP Integration

  • wab-mcp-adapter — Converts WAB actions into MCP tools
  • Compatible with Claude, GPT, LangChain, and any MCP-enabled agent

Fairness Engine

  • Neutrality scoring (0–100) for equitable site discovery
  • Anti-monopoly bias correction in search results
  • Commission transparency and local business support flags

WordPress Plugin

  • Discovery Protocol endpoints (REST API + rewrite rules)
  • NoScript meta tags and fallback for HTTP-only agents
  • Protocol status page in admin settings

Infrastructure

  • Dynamic subscription plan management via admin dashboard
  • OpenAPI specification for all public endpoints
  • llms.txt and llms-full.txt for AI model discovery
  • AI-optimized robots.txt and sitemap.xml

entry.title: v1.1.0 — Protocol Specification & MCP Adapter
entry.summary: Introduction of the WAB Protocol Specification v1.0, MCP adapter for LLM integration, Discovery Protocol, and Fairness Engine.

entry.title: v1.0.0 — Initial Release
entry.summary: First public release of Web Agent Bridge. Core runtime with AI command registration, client-side bridge script, Express.js server, license management, analytics, and the initial NoScript fallback.
